data.title: bug: healthcheck references identity paths that no longer exist after .skogai reorg
data.description:
## Problem

After today's reorg (commits `210787c` → `db781e2` on 2026-04-18), `claude-todo/bin/healthcheck` reports 20+ `[FAIL]` entries because the paths it checks moved or were removed.

## Reproduction

```bash
./claude-todo/bin/healthcheck
```

**Output excerpt:**

```
identity paths
------------------
  [FAIL] personal/soul/01-equation.md
  [FAIL] personal/soul/02-skogai-family.md
  ... (all 10 soul sections)
  [FAIL] personal/profile.md
  [FAIL] personal/journal/CONVENTIONS.md
  [FAIL] personal/core/certainty-principle.md
  [FAIL] personal/core/placeholder-approach.md
  [FAIL] personal/core/epistemic-frameworks.md
  [FAIL] personal/core/context-destruction.md
  [FAIL] personal/core/learnings.md

routing
------------------
  [FAIL] CLAUDE.md
  [FAIL] personal/CLAUDE.md
  [FAIL] personal/soul/CLAUDE.md
```

Exit code is still `0`, but the Stop hook wrapper (`settings.json` line \~100) only fires on non-zero exit — so the failures are silent.

## Root cause

The reorg moved things into `.skogai/` but healthcheck was not updated. Either:

* The old `personal/` tree is gone entirely (confirm), OR
* It still exists but at a new path (`.skogai/personal/`? not visible)

Also: repo root `CLAUDE.md` is still present — so that `[FAIL]` suggests healthcheck runs with the wrong `CWD` (likely `claude-todo/`, not repo root).

## Fix candidates

1. Update healthcheck to `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"` before checking paths
2. Update the path list to match the new structure
3. Make it return non-zero so the Stop hook surfaces the failures
4. Move healthcheck itself out of `claude-todo/` back to a canonical location (see related consolidation issue)

## Related

* [#25](https://linear.app/skogai/issue/SKO-160/feat-document-all-binariesscripts-into-claudemd) (document binaries)
* The reorg commits: `210787c`, `49d3a0a`, `0e565be`, `f687f3a`, `03a9d96`, `f37d4ba`, `e0db51d`, `db781e2`
